what_it_does: Defines the SCREEN_READER cognition role and its structured output schema for a guided-review walkthrough cast: it reads the HTML mockup injected into live-state via the mockup:// focus and explains, in plain language for a non-developer operator, what the screen is and what can be done there.
observations: Declares a Pydantic model ScreenReaderOut with three fields: screen_present, what_this_is, and what_you_can_do.
The ROLE dict bundles id, label, description, a detailed prompt_template, output_schema, input_addresses, trigger, model_binding, mode_scope, rules, and render_hint.
The prompt explicitly instructs the model not to describe HTML, tags, or code, and to return only JSON.
model_binding lists required capabilities ['chat', 'json'] but leaves default_model and default_base_url as None.
standouts: Explicitly built for a non-developer operator who reads no code.
Positioned as a declared cognition role on the cast-beyond-listening seam rather than ad-hoc injection.
Reads the mockup from live-state via the mockup:// focus, not via resolve_address.
conventions: Uses Pydantic BaseModel for structured LLM output.
Uses a declarative ROLE dictionary with standardized metadata keys.
Docstring includes purpose, scope, and a commit reference (56d42f4).
Uses a tuple for input_addresses and a set literal for mode_scope.
concerns: The prompt_template is a long inline string mixing instructions, schema description, and an example, making it harder to maintain or localize.
No runtime validation logic is present for whether the MOCKUP UNDER REVIEW block actually exists in live_state.
default_model and default_base_url are None, so resolution behavior is unspecified in this file.
rules is empty, leaving no explicit guardrails beyond the prompt text.
notes: This file is the declarative role definition for a screen-reading assistant that translates a design mockup into plain-language guidance for non-technical reviewers.
questions: How is the mockup:// focus injected into live_state and who is responsible for it?
What enforces that this role only fires in walkthrough mode and not in listening mode?
What component consumes render_hint and how does it use shape/lane?
How are default_model and default_base_url resolved when they are None?
What is the exact lifecycle of a 'walkthrough cast' and when is this role fired?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it defines a screen reader role and schema that explains a mockup in plain language for a non-developer, and that is exactly what the code declares.
apparent_themes: Separation of implementation details from user-facing explanation
intention_signals: It is trying to create a reusable, first-class cognition role that prevents the 'detached-studio failure' by giving non-developer operators a plain-language walkthrough of whatever mockup is under review.
novelty: A dedicated role whose sole responsibility is translating an injected HTML mockup into non-technical plain language, explicitly scoped to walkthrough mode and not the listening cast.
contribution: Provides a structured, non-technical explanation role and output schema that a fused system would keep to bridge design mockups and operator comprehension during guided reviews.
summary_for_embedding: roles/screen_reader.py defines the SCREEN_READER cognition role and its Pydantic output schema for walkthrough-mode review, translating an injected mockup:// HTML block into plain-language descriptions of what a screen is and what actions are available.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to give non-developer operators a declared, reusable screen-reading faculty during guided design walkthroughs.

symbols.does: Pydantic model that structures the screen reader's plain-language output.
symbols.description: Defines three fields—screen_present (boolean), what_this_is (string), and what_you_can_do (string)—to enforce a non-technical explanation of the mockup under review; it is referenced as output_schema in the ROLE dictionary.
